design.summary:
M16 = the LLM-agnostic inference BRIDGE: an agent invokes a model through a capability (Rights::INVOKE_MODEL, method M_MODEL_INVOKE=17) naming the target via a `model:` scheme; a safe in-kernel router binds a REGISTERED backend behind ONE uniform contract, backend identity hidden from the agent. Every M11 reservation already exists and the cap gate ALREADY bites (caps.rs:110/204/341/400) — M16 only ADDS a payload field, one dispatch arm, a mint helper, a new safe module, two facades, and the self-test. ZERO new unsafe (a stateless in-kernel MockBackend + an immutable const route table need no UnsafeCell). CUDA/real-API/vsock are explicitly deferred behind the SAME InferBackend trait. Concrete plan + asserts below; marker `M16: infer OK`.

VERIFIED PRECEDENT FILES (absolute):
- C:\Users\Arda\workspaces\@cogitave\tabos\crates\tb-hal\src\caps.rs — Object{kind,mem,scalar,chan,block} @231-247; M_BLOCK_READ arm @984 (the exact clone-Rc-out → match payload → BadCap-on-None template); 5 Rc::new(Object{...}) sites: mint@505, mint_memory_home@522, mint_record@538, mint_channel_endpoint@558, mint_block@582; block_of@600; required_right@391; dispatch fall-through `_ => SysReturn::ok(0)` @1003 (LAST arm, MUST be kept).
- crates\tb-hal\src\blocks.rs:1 / ipc.rs:1 — `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` safe-module header to copy verbatim.
- crates\tb-hal\src\lib.rs — mod decls @43-45 (`mod blocks;`@44); facades agent_channel_connect@1422, agent_block_create@1620, agent_block_map@1653, agent_block_dispatch@1690, agent_mint_generic@1510.
- kernel\src\main.rs — agent_c=MANIFEST_A@1378, agent_d=MANIFEST_B@1379 (both OMIT INVOKE_MODEL); M15 marker serial_write_str @~1887; final tb_hal::halt() @~1890.
design.new_unsafe:
ZERO new unsafe — confirmed against the actual change set. (a) infer.rs carries `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` (compiler-enforced) — its MockBackend is a stateless pure transform (trivially Sync, so `static MOCK_*` needs NO UnsafeCell), and ROUTES is an immutable `static &[&'static dyn InferBackend]` (no interior mutability). (b) caps.rs changes are a struct field, 5× `session: None`, a mint helper (Rc::new + alloc), and one dispatch arm that clones an Rc and matches an Option — pure safe Rust, the M_BLOCK_READ template. (c) lib.rs facades use the PRE-EXISTING AGENTS.slot().as_mut() access (the same pattern agent_block_create/agent_channel_connect already use) — not a NEW unsafe block. The single existing unsafe surface (the AGENTS UnsafeCell cell + arch) is untouched; M16 adds none.

EXPLICITLY DEFERRED behind the SAME InferBackend contract (out of the M16 DoD): real Anthropic/OpenAI adapters (userspace, net egress, EMIT_EXTERNAL-gated, translate neutral↔Messages/Chat-Completions); a real local backend over vsock to the IOMMU-confined Linux GPU driver-VM (Qubes/Xen dom0 pattern). CUDA + the closed NVIDIA kernel module + GSP firmware live ENTIRELY inside that quarantined VM, reached ONLY over one hypervisor-mediated vsock CID:port; the sovereign kernel NEVER links CUDA, never speaks wire JSON, never sees GPU memory. The ONLY future unsafe is the tb-hal virtio-vsock ring (MMIO/PCI probe, volatile descriptor ring, doorbell, completion IRQ through M8, tokens streamed over an ordered M14 stream) — a single audited shim introduced only when the vsock backend lands, L2-sovereignty track. The M16 marker block touches none of it (inline-scalar contract only). Swapping Claude for a local llama is a REGISTRY edit, not an agent-code change — the LLM-agnostic pillar made concrete.
risks: Object grows a field: ALL 5 Rc::new(Object{...}) sites (caps.rs mint@505/mint_memory_home@522/mint_record@538/mint_channel_endpoint@558/mint_block@582) MUST add `session: None` or the crate won't compile — mechanical, same as M15's `block: None`; a hard compile error, not a silent bug.

ADD the M_MODEL_INVOKE arm BEFORE `_ => SysReturn::ok(0)` (caps.rs:1003) — do NOT replace the fall-through. The stub still serves M_AGENT_SPAWN(16)/M_MEM_WRITE_PROC(18)/M_EMIT_EXTERNAL(21)/M_BUDGET_DELEGATE(22)/M_BLOCK_MAP(28)/M_HANDLE_TRANSFER(3); replacing it would regress those rights-checked stubs.

ZERO-unsafe depends on keeping the M16 registry IMMUTABLE (a const `&'static` slice over stateless statics). A runtime-mutable router for real vsock providers needs the AGENTS-style UnsafeCell+unsafe impl Sync = NEW unsafe — that is the deferred dynamic path; do NOT add it for the marker.

MockBackend MUST stay pure/deterministic (no clock, no rng, no I/O) or the CI marker is non-reproducible across QEMU runs — keep `infer` a closed transform/echo of the inline prompt scalar (e.g. prompt ^ 0xA110_C0DE).
InferBackend methods must stay generic-free so `&'static dyn InferBackend` / `Rc<dyn InferBackend>` are object-safe in no_std+alloc; a generic `infer<T>` breaks dyn.

Least-privilege shifts to the FACADE: agent_model_open mints the session WITH INVOKE_MODEL directly (MANIFEST_A/B omit it), so the gate now depends on the facade choosing rights correctly — KEEP the Denied self-test (narrow-to-drop INVOKE_MODEL -> Denied) to prove the gate still bites (the M13 home_ro / M14 epa_no_w precedent).

Session-open as a kernel FACADE (not a dispatch method) is correct (dispatch holds only &mut HandleTable, can't reach the Router) but means parse+route is NOT itself cap-gated by dispatch — the gate lands at INVOKE time on the minted handle. Ensure an unknown-token/unknown-scheme open returns a CLEAN closed status (BadCap), never a panic, so a bad scheme can't crash the kernel.

Do NOT add M_MODEL_OPEN/M_MODEL_CLOSE method numbers: reuse the existing meta-ops (M_HANDLE_CLOSE/REVOKE/TRANSFER drop the owned session via slot-vacate). New numbers would mutate the M11 closed method set and invalidate the existing BadMethod self-tests (the 32/33 probes).

The real virtio/vsock transport + completion-IRQ-through-M8 + streamed tokens over an M14 stream is the ONLY part that adds unsafe (the virtio ring) and is correctly DEFERRED to the L2-sovereignty/driver-VM track — confirm the M16 marker block stays inline-scalar and touches none of it.

Make `infer` a `pub mod` (like `pub mod caps;`) so the self-test reaches tb_hal::infer::parse_scheme and the contract types are the public adapter API; keep MockBackend/ROUTES/statics private inside the module to avoid leaking the in-kernel mock as public surface.

recon.model_invoke_seam: THE seam is the single M11 chokepoint `caps::dispatch` (crates/tb-hal/src/caps.rs). M_MODEL_INVOKE=17 is ALREADY registered and ALREADY gated: `required_right(M_MODEL_INVOKE) => Rights::INVOKE_MODEL` (caps.rs ~line where required_right maps methods). Today M_MODEL_INVOKE has NO explicit match arm in `dispatch`, so it falls through to the generic rights-checked stub `_ => SysReturn::ok(0)` (the LAST arm of the dispatch match). The gate already works end-to-end: a handle lacking INVOKE_MODEL returns Denied, an unknown method returns BadMethod — these are inherited for free from the existing `required_right` + `live` + `rights.contains(need)` prologue. M16 work = REPLACE the stub fall-through with a real M_MODEL_INVOKE arm that mirrors the M_BLOCK_WRITE/M_BLOCK_READ arms EXACTLY: clone the `Rc<Object>` out of the slot (drop the slot borrow first — the single-&mut-at-a-time discipline), read the new `session` payload, and if `Some(sess)` call `sess.invoke(args.args)` -> SysReturn::ok(response_scalar); if the object carries no session payload (a non-session cap presented to a model method) -> SysReturn::err(SysStatus::BadCap), the identical "wrong-payload" branch M_BLOCK_WRITE uses. NO new method number, NO new SysStatus, NO new unsafe — the invoke body reaches the backend purely through the Object's payload Rc, never a global router (the router is consulted ONCE at session-open time, not per-invoke), so dispatch keeps holding only `&mut HandleTable`. This is the precise M_BLOCK_WRITE precedent (record-plane CAS reached via the Object's `block: Option<Rc<Block>>`).
recon.model_scheme: The `model:` scheme is the LLM-agnosticity primitive (docs/ARCHITECTURE.md line 71: "model:anthropic/opus and model:local/llama are two provider daemons of the same contract — LLM-agnosticity = who registered the scheme"; ROADMAP-V2.md M16 line 253-256: kernel ROUTER binds whichever provider registered the scheme, caller never names a provider). On the pointer-free [u64;4] SyscallArgs the scheme STRING cannot ride a register, so the binding is two-phase (mirrors how handles are opaque u64 tokens for objects whose real identity lives kernel-side): (1) PARSE+ROUTE at session-open: a safe `infer::parse_scheme(&str) -> Option<ModelId>` lives in the new infer.rs and a `Router`/registry maps a small model-id TOKEN (a u64 the agent carries as an immediate, e.g. MODEL_CLAUDE=1, MODEL_LOCAL_LLAMA3=2) to a registered `&'static dyn InferBackend`. The literal scheme strings (`model:claude`, `model:local/llama3`, `model:vendor/name`) live in the registry table next to each backend; the agent never holds the string, only the opaque token — exactly like a Handle is an opaque token for a kernel-side Object. (2) INVOKE rides the resulting ModelSession HANDLE (capability), so the scheme is parsed exactly once and the bound backend identity is HIDDEN from the agent thereafter (swap the registered backend behind the same token -> identical agent code path = the backend-agnostic contract). The self-test demonstrates parse (`parse_scheme("model:local/llama3")`==Some, `parse_scheme("memory:x")`==None), route (known token -> mock backend), and unknown-scheme (an unregistered token -> Router returns None -> a CLEAN closed error, no panic). The full MCP `{cost,speed,intelligence}` preference vector + typed inference DAG (ROADMAP M16) is a richer InferRequest carried later over an M14 channel / M15 block — deferred behind the same trait, exactly as M14 deferred copy_to_user.
recon.backend_trait: New file crates/tb-hal/src/infer.rs carrying `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` (the exact mem.rs/ipc.rs/blocks.rs safe-module header — all four are 100% safe). It defines: (a) the CONTRACT types `InferRequest { prompt: u64, /* args[1..3] reserved for the deferred byte prompt over a block/channel */ }` and `InferResponse { token: u64 }` (inline-scalar at M16, the M14 Message::payload precedent; real variable-length byte prompt/response is the deferred unsafe-free copy_to_user/block-byte path). (b) The object-safe trait `pub trait InferBackend: Sync { fn infer(&self, req: &InferRequest) -> InferResponse; fn scheme(&self) -> &'static str; }` — no generic methods so `&'static dyn InferBackend` / `Rc<dyn InferBackend>` is legal in no_std+alloc. (c) `pub struct MockBackend` — a STATELESS, deterministic loopback (e.g. `infer` returns a pure transform of `req.prompt` such as `prompt ^ 0xA110_C0DE` or an echo) with NO clock/rng (QEMU has no real net/GPU, so determinism is mandatory for a CI marker); being stateless it is trivially `Sync`, so a `static MOCK: MockBackend = MockBackend::new();` is sound with ZERO unsafe. (d) `pub struct Router` / a `const ROUTES: &[(ModelId, &'static dyn InferBackend)]` immutable table — `resolve(token) -> Option<&'static dyn InferBackend>`. Registration of the in-kernel mock = adding the `static MOCK` to this const/static `&'static` table: fully safe, no UnsafeCell needed because the mock is immutable+Sync. (Runtime/userspace provider registration — a mutable router for real vsock-backed providers — rides the existing AGENTS-style `UnsafeCell + unsafe impl Sync` cell OR a RefCell behind the single-core-interrupts-masked discipline; that is the deferred dynamic path and the ONLY place a future unsafe could appear, kept out of the M16 mock DoD.)
recon.session_object: ObjKind::ModelSession ALREADY exists (caps.rs ObjKind enum, the 2nd variant, discriminant 1, currently identity-only/reserved — "A model inference session (M16)"). Give the Object the payload, mirroring `block: Option<Rc<blocks::Block>>`: add ONE field to `struct Object` (caps.rs line 231) — `session: Option<crate::infer::ModelSession>` — where `infer::ModelSession { backend: &'static dyn InferBackend, model: ModelId }` (plus an optional interior-mutable `Cell<u64>` turn counter if a stateful session lifecycle is wanted; not required for the deterministic marker). Unlike Channel/Block (shared across two tables, hence `Rc`), a ModelSession is single-owner so it can be OWNED inline (the `mem: Option<RefCell<MemSubstrate>>` ownership precedent), no Rc-of-session needed. LIFECYCLE: minted by the kernel facade `agent_model_open` (NOT a dispatch method — it needs the Router, which dispatch can't reach; this mirrors `agent_channel_connect`/`agent_block_create` which are kernel-mediated facades that mint endpoints/blocks directly with chosen rights, bypassing the manifest). It mints the ModelSession bound to the router-resolved backend WITH Rights::INVOKE_MODEL|READ directly into the agent's table. Reached ONLY through its capability via the M11 chokepoint (M_MODEL_INVOKE). FREED by the existing meta-ops with ZERO new code: M_HANDLE_CLOSE / M_HANDLE_REVOKE / M_HANDLE_TRANSFER vacate the slot and drop the owned session (Zircon refcount semantics) — so NO M_MODEL_CLOSE method number is needed and the closed method set is UNCHANGED. New helpers in caps.rs mirror the block helpers exactly: `mint_model_session(rights, infer::ModelSession) -> Option<Handle>` (the `mint_block` clone) and, only if the facade needs to re-resolve, `model_of(h) -> Result<(Rights, &ModelSession), SysStatus>` (the `block_of` clone). The 5 existing `Rc::new(Object {...})` constructor sites (caps.rs lines 507/525/541/564/587 — mint, mint_memory_home, mint_record, mint_channel_endpoint, mint_block) each gain `session: None`, exactly as `block: None` was appended everywhere when M15 landed.
recon.abi: Pointer-free, ABI-stable, no new numbers. INVOKE: SyscallArgs { method: M_MODEL_INVOKE (=17, already reserved), handle: <ModelSession capability holding INVOKE_MODEL>, args: [prompt_scalar, 0, 0, 0] } -> SysReturn { Ok, response_scalar } where response_scalar = the bound backend's deterministic InferResponse.token. Gate: INVOKE_MODEL via the existing required_right(17)==Rights::INVOKE_MODEL — a handle without it returns Denied from the dispatch prologue (no per-arm code). Non-session object with INVOKE_MODEL -> BadCap (payload-None branch). Unknown method (e.g. 33) -> BadMethod (closed set intact, unchanged). SESSION-OPEN is kernel-mediated (facade, not an ABI method), so the agent names the model via the model-id TOKEN passed to `agent_model_open(task, model_token)` -> (status, session_handle_raw); an unregistered token -> a clean closed error (BadCap/a no-backend status), never a panic. This reuses the M14 inline-scalar-payload discipline verbatim: the real variable-length BYTE prompt/response (and the {cost,speed,intelligence} preference DAG) ride a future M14 channel / M15 block and are DEFERRED, the same deferral as M14 copy_to_user and M15 frame-byte I/O — so the M16 marker rides inline scalars at ZERO new unsafe. The [u64;4] SyscallArgs struct, SysStatus closed enum, and the method-number space are all untouched (the M11 frozen-boundary invariant is preserved — only a payload field + a dispatch arm body change).
recon.selftest_plan: Insert the M16 block in kernel/src/main.rs BETWEEN line 1888 (`tb_hal::serial_write_str("M15: blocks OK\n");`) and line 1890 (`tb_hal::halt()`) — the natural end of the rust_main boot self-test, reusing agent_c/agent_d already spawned at function scope for M13/M14/M15 (no extra MAX_TASKS slots). Pattern = the M13/M14/M15 blocks verbatim: a local `fn m16_fail(why)->!` (serial "M16: FAIL "+why+halt), `let ok/den/badcap/badmethod = SysStatus::* as u32`. Steps: (0) `static MOCK: infer::MockBackend` registered in the const router table under TWO tokens MODEL_CLAUDE and MODEL_LOCAL_LLAMA3 — proving two `model:` names bind ONE contract. (1) PARSE: assert infer::parse_scheme("model:local/llama3")==Some && parse_scheme("memory:x")==None. (2) OPEN+INVOKE: `agent_model_open(agent_c, MODEL_CLAUDE)` -> Ok + session handle (minted WITH INVOKE_MODEL); `agent_model_invoke(agent_c, sess, PROMPT)` -> Ok + assert response == MockBackend's deterministic transform of PROMPT. (3) BACKEND-AGNOSTIC: open a SECOND session naming MODEL_LOCAL_LLAMA3 (different `model:` scheme, same mock contract); invoke the SAME PROMPT -> SAME deterministic response — same agent code, swapped backend identity. (4) DENIED (missing right): drive M_HANDLE_NARROW on the session to drop INVOKE_MODEL (the M14 epa_no_w / M13 home_ro precedent), then invoke the narrowed handle -> Denied. (5) UNKNOWN scheme: `agent_model_open(agent_c, UNKNOWN_TOKEN)` -> a clean no-backend error status (not Ok, not a panic). (6) NON-SESSION: M_MODEL_INVOKE via agent_block_dispatch/agent_model_dispatch on a Generic cap minted WITH INVOKE_MODEL -> BadCap (payload-None branch). (7) closed-set probe: an unknown method stays BadMethod. Then `tb_hal::serial_write_str("M16: infer OK\n");` (the exact DoD marker from ROADMAP-V2.md line 62), then the existing `tb_hal::halt()`. Each assert routes through `caps::dispatch` against the agent's OWN table (the M11 chokepoint), proving gate+route+contract+lifecycle. Timer is already disarmed (single-core, interrupts masked) before this point — the discipline the safe payloads rely on.
recon.integration_points:
FILES (absolute):
(NEW) C:\Users\Arda\workspaces\@cogitave\tabos\crates\tb-hal\src\infer.rs — `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` safe module: InferRequest/InferResponse contract types, `trait InferBackend: Sync`, `struct MockBackend`, `Router`/const ROUTES table + parse_scheme, `struct ModelSession{backend,model}`. Sibling of mem.rs/ipc.rs/blocks.rs.
C:\Users\Arda\workspaces\@cogitave\tabos\crates\tb-hal\src\caps.rs — (a) struct Object @ line 231: add `session: Option<crate::infer::ModelSession>`; (b) the 5 `Rc::new(Object{...})` sites @ lines 507,525,541,564,587: add `session: None`; (c) add `mint_model_session` (clone of `mint_block`) and optional `model_of` (clone of `block_of`); (d) replace the dispatch `_ => SysReturn::ok(0)` fall-through with a real M_MODEL_INVOKE arm (clone Rc<Object> out, match `session`, call backend, BadCap on None) — the M_BLOCK_WRITE precedent. M_MODEL_INVOKE=17 + required_right(17)=>INVOKE_MODEL + ObjKind::ModelSession + Rights::INVOKE_MODEL(bit 5, =Rights(1<<5)) ALL already exist — no ABI change.
C:\Users\Arda\workspaces\@cogitave\tabos\crates\tb-hal\src\lib.rs — (a) module decls @ lines 40-47: add `mod infer;` after line 44 (`mod blocks;`); (b) facade region (after `agent_block_member_writable`, ends ~line 1727): add `agent_model_open(task, model_token) -> Option<(u32,u64)>` (mirrors `agent_channel_connect`/`agent_block_create`: resolve token via Router, mint_model_session WITH INVOKE_MODEL into the agent's table, clean error on unknown token) and `agent_model_invoke(task, sess, prompt)` + `agent_model_dispatch(task, method, handle, a0)` (the `agent_block_dispatch` clone driving caps::dispatch against the agent's OWN table). The set_trap_hook/IRQ_HOOK precedent (lines 161-194 / 706-735) is the kernel-hook pattern but is DELIBERATELY NOT USED for M16 (its transmute is unsafe) — the const `&'static` router table replaces it with zero unsafe.
C:\Users\Arda\workspaces\@cogitave\tabos\kernel\src\main.rs — insert M16 self-test block between line 1888 (M15 marker) and 1890 (halt); reuse agent_c/agent_d; emit `M16: infer OK`. Manifests MANIFEST_A/B (lines ~187,209) intentionally OMIT INVOKE_MODEL — the success path's session is minted WITH INVOKE_MODEL by the facade (kernel-mediated grant), the Denied path narrows it away; no manifest change required.
DOCS authority: docs/ROADMAP-V2.md lines 62 (marker), 253-267 (M16 spec), 332 (decision 8: mock provider); docs/ARCHITECTURE.md lines 71,101-102 (scheme=who-registered, kernel-router-binds-backend).
risks: Object struct grows a field: ALL 5 `Rc::new(Object{...})` constructors (caps.rs 507/525/541/564/587) must add `session: None` or the crate will not compile — mechanical, same as when M15 appended `block: None` everywhere; missing one is a hard compile error not a silent bug.

ZERO-unsafe constraint on the registry: a RUNTIME-mutable router (for real userspace/vsock providers) would need the AGENTS-style `UnsafeCell + unsafe impl Sync` cell = NEW unsafe. Avoid by keeping the M16 mock registry an IMMUTABLE const `&'static [(ModelId,&'static dyn InferBackend)]` table over a stateless `static MOCK` (Sync-trivial). Dynamic registration is the deferred path; do NOT add it for the marker.
MockBackend MUST be pure/deterministic (no clock, no rng, no I/O) or the CI marker is non-reproducible across QEMU runs — keep `infer` a closed transform/echo of the inline prompt scalar.

dyn-trait object-safety: InferBackend methods must stay generic-free so `&'static dyn InferBackend` and `Rc<dyn InferBackend>` are legal in no_std+alloc; a generic `infer<T>` would break object safety.

Rights leak risk: agent_model_open mints the session WITH INVOKE_MODEL directly (manifests omit it), so the least-privilege story now depends on the FACADE choosing rights correctly rather than the manifest — keep the Denied self-test (narrow-to-drop INVOKE_MODEL -> Denied) to prove the gate still bites, mirroring the M13 home_ro / M14 epa_no_w precedents.

Session-open as a kernel FACADE (not a dispatch method) is correct (it needs the Router, dispatch holds only &mut HandleTable — the agent_channel_connect/agent_block_create precedent), but it means the `model:` parse+route is NOT itself capability-gated by dispatch; the gate lands at INVOKE time on the minted session handle. Ensure unknown-token open returns a CLEAN closed status (BadCap), never a panic, so a bad scheme cannot crash the kernel.

Do NOT add a new method number for session-open or close: reuse the existing meta-ops (M_HANDLE_CLOSE/REVOKE/TRANSFER drop the owned session via slot vacate). Adding M_MODEL_OPEN/M_MODEL_CLOSE would mutate the M11 closed method set and invalidate the existing BadMethod self-tests (probes of 32/33/99).

The real virtio/vsock transport + completion-IRQ-through-M8 + streaming tokens over an M14 stream (ROADMAP M16) is the ONLY part that adds unsafe (the virtio ring); it is correctly DEFERRED to the L2-sovereignty/driver-VM track — confirm the M16 marker block touches none of it (inline-scalar contract only).
